channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: “I am pleased to announce that former Congressman Billy Long, of the Great State of Missouri, will be appointed to serve as the Commissioner of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS),” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: 2 students injured, suspected gunman dead in shooting at Northern California school
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Searchers don't expect to find woman in sinkhole alive: Pennsylvania police
item.description: “We’ve had no signs of any form of life or anything,” Pennsylvania State Police spokesperson Trooper Steve Limani said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to onions served at McDonald's is over, health officials say
item.description: Federal health officials have concluded their investigation into a multi-state E. coli outbreak tied to contaminated slivered onions previously served on McDonald’s Quarter Pounder burgers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Hunter Biden gun case terminated after President Joe Biden's sweeping pardon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge terminated the gun case against Hunter Biden on Tuesday after President Joe Biden issued a sweeping pardon for his son. U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika closed the case the week before Hunter Biden was to be sentenced.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on medical treatments for transgender minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, the Supreme Court on Wednesday seemed likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump moves to toss Georgia criminal case as president-elect
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday asked to throw out his criminal election interference charges in Georgia following his election victory. Trump’s two federal indictments have already been tossed, and the former president is attempting to do the same in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Key things to know about the fatal attack on the UnitedHealthcare CEO in NYC
item.description: The chief executive of UnitedHealthcare, one of the nation’s largest insurers, was killed in midtown Manhattan in what police described as a targeted attack by a shooter outside a hotel where the company was holding a conference.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: If you had a Patreon account, you may qualify for this payment
item.description: Patreon will pay $7.25 million across users who had accounts and accessed videos on Patreon.com between April 1, 2016 and Sept. 23, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio school to offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: A new religious release program for students is entering public schools in Marysville, Ohio, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, KRON4

item.title: With dad on deployment, business decorates his family's home for holidays
item.description: Dawn Baslee, mother of three, told NewsNation affiliate WAVY this is the second Christmas her husband will miss with family because of a deployment.
item.dc:creator: Madison Pearman, NewsNation

item.title: Puppy food sold in 7 states recalled due to salmonella contamination: FDA
item.description: A litter of puppies became ill after eating the mix, a customer told the Virginia Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, Fox 5

item.title: Manhattan DA's downplaying of Daniel Penny's potential punishment 'improper and misleading': defense
item.description: What's behind Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg's push to downplay the potential consequences for Daniel Penny, if his office gets a conviction in the Jordan Neely case?
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida man intentionally drove truck into USAA building: Police
item.description: Robert Beaty, 70, was denied entry to the building when he arrived at around 2:12 p.m. and demanded to speak to a USAA representative.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: “He also recognizes that digital assets & other innovations are crucial to Making America Greater than Ever Before,” the president-elect added.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO kept a low public profile. Then he was shot to death in New York
item.description: Brian Thompson led one of the biggest health insurers in the US but was unknown to millions of people his decisions affected.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California farmers are hopeful Trump administration will deliver more water to fields
item.description: Since winning the election, President-elect Donald Trump has been talking about immigration, border security and government efficiency. But in California farm country, his comments about water are also getting top attention.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk is Trump's pick to lead NASA
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has tapped a tech billionaire who performed the first private spacewalk to lead NASA. Trump announced on Wednesday his choice for NASA administrator.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump team outlines inauguration benefits for donors
item.description: The team highlighted that committee members who give $1 million or raise $2 million receive six tickets to access exclusive inaugural events between Saturday, Jan. 18, and Monday, Jan. 20, the day of the swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: British indie rock band robbed at gunpoint in California '10 minutes into US tour,' group says
item.description: Sports Team, a six-piece London-based alternative rock band, had stopped for coffee when someone alerted them that their van was being ransacked by robbers, according to their latest Instagram post.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in 'targeted attack' in NYC, officials say
item.description: The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a Midtown shooting Wednesday morning, sources have confirmed to Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: A judge is weighing which of Missouri's sweeping abortion restrictions will fall under a new constitutional amendment.
item.dc:creator: Summer Ballentine And Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, ABC 4

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to McDonald's onions is over: CDC
item.description: An outbreak across 14 states in October connected to McDonald’s slivered onions from the popular menu item impacted at least 100 people.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, KRON4

item.title: Missouri executes a man for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl in 2007
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — Rowan Ford knew Christopher Collings as “Uncle Chris” after he spent several months living with her family. On Tuesday, Collings was executed for sexually assaulting and killing the child, then dumping her body in a sinkhole outside a small Missouri town.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Freedom is permanent for Missourian described as the longest-held wrongly incarcerated woman in US
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Years of legal wrangling have come to an end for a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, Fox 5

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, KRON4

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: These are the worst cities for package theft in 2024
item.description: The holiday season, which has already seen a surge in online shopping, presents even more opportunities for package thieves.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5
